title: Lasiocorys poggeana
taxonomy: Lasiocorys poggeana is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. It is native to tropical Africa. The classification of Lasiocorys poggeana is as follows: its kingdom is Plantae, its class is Magnoliopsida, its order is Myrtales, its family is Melastomataceae, and its genus and species are Lasiocorys and poggeana respectively. This plant is often found in moist areas and is known for its vibrant flowers. The specific classification may vary depending on the source and the most recent updates in botanical taxonomy.

intro: Lasiocorys poggeana, also known as Pogge's lasiocorys, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, native to tropical Africa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Lasiocorys poggeana include alkaloids, terpenoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Lasiocorys poggeana is often prepared as a decoction, tincture, or infused in hot water to make a tea, allowing for its bioactive constituents to be effectively extracted and utilized.
